facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement Config (src/config.py)
Centralized configuration management. Holds constants like window dimensions,
facts.lesson: Centralize configuration in a dedicated module to ensure single-source-of-truth access and eliminate scattered hardcoded values across the codebase.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement Config (src/config.py)
Centralized configuration management. Holds constants like window dimensions,
facts.lesson: Use type-hinted, immutable constants in configuration modules to prevent runtime side effects and improve IDE autocompletion for development teams.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement Config (src/config.py)
Centralized configuration management. Holds constants like window dimensions,
facts.lesson: Externalize sensitive environment variables from config files using standard libraries like `os.getenv` or `pydantic-settings` to avoid committing credentials to version control.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement LoggerService (src/services/logger.py)
Captures application runtime errors and logs state transitions
facts.lesson: Encapsulate standard logging in a service class to abstract log rotation, file formatting, and directory initialization, preventing fragmented configuration across high-precision calculation modules.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement LoggerService (src/services/logger.py)
Captures application runtime errors and logs state transitions
facts.lesson: Ensure log directory existence before instantiation; use `pathlib.Path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` to avoid FileNotFoundError during startup in containerized environments.

facts.task:

Custom button widgets. Manages click events and visual feedback for the
facts.lesson: Encapsulate UI widget styling within custom classes inheriting from tkinter.Button to ensure consistent theme application and centralized event handling via the command pattern.

PHASE 1: Implement ButtonUI (src/ui/buttons.py)
Custom button widgets. Manages click events and visual feedback for the
facts.lesson: Implement explicit state management methods like set_active in custom widgets to provide immediate visual feedback during asynchronous state transitions or event triggers.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement DisplayUI (src/ui/display.py)
Wraps tkinter Text/Entry widgets. Includes horizontal scrolling for lar
facts.lesson: Use tkinter's xscrollcommand linked to a Scrollbar widget to handle overflowing text in Entry fields, ensuring visibility of long numerical outputs without truncation or UI breakage.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement DisplayUI (src/ui/display.py)
Wraps tkinter Text/Entry widgets. Includes horizontal scrolling for lar
facts.lesson: Centralize UI dimension constants in a dedicated config module rather than hardcoding values in view classes to maintain consistent layout behavior across the application.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement LoggerService (src/services/logger.py)
Captures application runtime errors and logs state transitions
facts.lesson: Use the logging module's file rotation (RotatingFileHandler) to prevent uncontrolled disk growth in production environments.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement LoggerService (src/services/logger.py)
Captures application runtime errors and logs state transitions
facts.lesson: Always capture full tracebacks using exc_info=True when logging errors to ensure debugging precision for high-stakes calculations.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement LoggerService (src/services/logger.py)
Captures application runtime errors and logs state transitions
facts.lesson: Implement thread-safe logging or process-safe file handling to avoid log corruption when multiple service instances write to the same output destination.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement CalcModel (src/models/calculator.py)
Manages calculator state using Decimal. Includes serialization m
facts.lesson: Use the decimal module's context for precision control instead of global rounding to prevent accumulated arithmetic drift in financial or high-precision states.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement CalcModel (src/models/calculator.py)
Manages calculator state using Decimal. Includes serialization m
facts.lesson: Implement explicit from_dict/to_dict methods with type validation using Pydantic or type hints to ensure state serializability when persisting calculator models.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement CalcModel (src/models/calculator.py)
Manages calculator state using Decimal. Includes serialization m
facts.lesson: Enforce strict setter validation for numerical inputs; raise ValueError immediately to keep state transitions atomic and maintain internal model integrity.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement MathEngine (src/services/math_engine.py)
High-precision arithmetic using decimal.Context. Handles div
facts.lesson: Use decimal.Context to enforce global precision settings in financial or scientific applications, ensuring consistency across disparate arithmetic operations.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement MathEngine (src/services/math_engine.py)
High-precision arithmetic using decimal.Context. Handles div
facts.lesson: Explicitly catch decimal.DivisionByZero and decimal.InvalidOperation to prevent unhandled arithmetic crashes; always log the context state before re-raising or returning error results.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement MainLayout (src/ui/layout.py)
Organizes the display and button grid. Provides the main window interfa
facts.lesson: Expose UI components as public interface contracts to decouple view logic from controller orchestration, ensuring the main window acts as a central registry for layout components.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement MainLayout (src/ui/layout.py)
Organizes the display and button grid. Provides the main window interfa
facts.lesson: Validate UI layout structures using headful test runners before integration to catch geometry management errors and layout inconsistencies early in the implementation phase.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement StringSanitizer (src/services/sanitizer.py)
Handles numeric string sanitization. Strips whitespace, c
facts.lesson: Use regex pre-validation with a deterministic fallback value to prevent downstream MathEngine crashes from malformed numeric strings.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement StringSanitizer (src/services/sanitizer.py)
Handles numeric string sanitization. Strips whitespace, c
facts.lesson: Normalize numeric locales by consistently replacing comma decimal separators with dots before parsing, ensuring compatibility with standard float processing libraries.

facts.task:

Handles numeric string sanitization. Strips whitespace, c
facts.lesson: Centralize error logging within service classes to decouple sanitization logic from observability infrastructure, simplifying debugging for future pipeline integrations.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement Controller (src/controller.py)
Orchestrates UI and model. Bridges input handling, mathematical execut
facts.lesson: Decouple business logic from UI components by injecting the UI interface into the Controller via dependency injection; this ensures the Controller remains framework-agnostic and testable.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement Controller (src/controller.py)
Orchestrates UI and model. Bridges input handling, mathematical execut
facts.lesson: Sanitize all raw inputs at the Controller boundary before passing them to the execution layer to prevent injection attacks and runtime math errors.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement Controller (src/controller.py)
Orchestrates UI and model. Bridges input handling, mathematical execut
facts.lesson: Implement a centralized state manager within the Controller to ensure UI displays remain synchronized with mathematical outputs, preventing UI/model state divergence.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement AppEntry (src/main.py)
Application root. Implements a DI container to instantiate the model, engine,
facts.lesson: Implement Dependency Injection at the entry point to decouple object creation from execution, ensuring services like Model and Controller are instantiated before the UI event loop starts.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement AppEntry (src/main.py)
Application root. Implements a DI container to instantiate the model, engine,
facts.lesson: Avoid global state in UI applications by passing instantiated controllers and models explicitly into view constructors, facilitating unit testing of individual layers without requiring a full GUI environment.

facts.task:
PHASE 1: Implement AppEntry (src/main.py)
Application root. Implements a DI container to instantiate the model, engine,
facts.lesson: Standardize logging initialization at the main entry point to ensure consistent observability across the model, engine, and controller layers throughout the application lifecycle.

nodes.lesson: Centralize configuration in a dedicated module to ensure single-source-of-truth access and eliminate scattered hardcoded values across the codebase.
Use type-hinted, immutable constants in configuration modules to prevent runtime side effects and improve IDE autocompletion for development teams.
Externalize sensitive environment variables from config files using standard libraries like `os.getenv` or `pydantic-settings` to avoid committing credentials to version control.
Encapsulate standard logging in a service class to abstract log rotation, file formatting, and directory initialization, preventing fragmented configuration across high-precision calculation modules.
Ensure log directory existence before instantiation; use `pathlib.Path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)` to avoid FileNotFoundError during startup in containerized environments.
Encapsulate UI widget styling within custom classes inheriting from tkinter.Button to ensure consistent theme application and centralized event handling via the command pattern.
Externalize UI configuration constants into a dedicated module to prevent hardcoded values and simplify styling updates across the layout component hierarchy.
Implement explicit state management methods like set_active in custom widgets to provide immediate visual feedback during asynchronous state transitions or event triggers.
Use tkinter's xscrollcommand linked to a Scrollbar widget to handle overflowing text in Entry fields, ensuring visibility of long numerical outputs without truncation or UI breakage.
Centralize UI dimension constants in a dedicated config module rather than hardcoding values in view classes to maintain consistent layout behavior across the application.
Use the logging module's file rotation (RotatingFileHandler) to prevent uncontrolled disk growth in production environments.
Always capture full tracebacks using exc_info=True when logging errors to ensure debugging precision for high-stakes calculations.
Implement thread-safe logging or process-safe file handling to avoid log corruption when multiple service instances write to the same output destination.
Use the decimal module's context for precision control instead of global rounding to prevent accumulated arithmetic drift in financial or high-precision states.
Implement explicit from_dict/to_dict methods with type validation using Pydantic or type hints to ensure state serializability when persisting calculator models.
Enforce strict setter validation for numerical inputs; raise ValueError immediately to keep state transitions atomic and maintain internal model integrity.
Use decimal.Context to enforce global precision settings in financial or scientific applications, ensuring consistency across disparate arithmetic operations.
Explicitly catch decimal.DivisionByZero and decimal.InvalidOperation to prevent unhandled arithmetic crashes; always log the context state before re-raising or returning error results.
Expose UI components as public interface contracts to decouple view logic from controller orchestration, ensuring the main window acts as a central registry for layout components.
Validate UI layout structures using headful test runners before integration to catch geometry management errors and layout inconsistencies early in the implementation phase.
Use regex pre-validation with a deterministic fallback value to prevent downstream MathEngine crashes from malformed numeric strings.
Normalize numeric locales by consistently replacing comma decimal separators with dots before parsing, ensuring compatibility with standard float processing libraries.
Centralize error logging within service classes to decouple sanitization logic from observability infrastructure, simplifying debugging for future pipeline integrations.
Decouple business logic from UI components by injecting the UI interface into the Controller via dependency injection; this ensures the Controller remains framework-agnostic and testable.
Sanitize all raw inputs at the Controller boundary before passing them to the execution layer to prevent injection attacks and runtime math errors.
Implement a centralized state manager within the Controller to ensure UI displays remain synchronized with mathematical outputs, preventing UI/model state divergence.
Implement Dependency Injection at the entry point to decouple object creation from execution, ensuring services like Model and Controller are instantiated before the UI event loop starts.

Avoid global state in UI applications by passing instantiated controllers and models explicitly into view constructors, facilitating unit testing of individual layers without requiring a full GUI environment.
Standardize logging initialization at the main entry point to ensure consistent observability across the model, engine, and controller layers throughout the application lifecycle.
